text: Elite endurance athletes distribute approximately 75% of training sessions in zone 1 (below VT1), 6-8% in zone 2 (between VT1 and VT2), and 15-20% in zone 3 (above VT2), following a polarized pattern.
Heart rate-based and session RPE-based methods produce similar training intensity distributions, making session RPE a valid alternative for quantifying training load distribution.
Elite endurance athletes train surprisingly little at lactate threshold intensity, with only 6-8% of sessions falling in the zone between VT1 and VT2.
Approximately 71% of training sessions are performed at blood lactate concentrations of 2.0 mM or below, confirming that the majority of endurance training occurs at low intensity.
When high-intensity sessions are performed in polarized training, they are done at substantial intensity — sessions above the lactate accommodation zone averaged 9.5 mM blood lactate.
specific_value: mean 9.5 ± 2.8 mM blood lactate for high-intensity sessions

text: The polarized training distribution pattern is consistent across observational studies of elite endurance athletes in multiple sports, suggesting it is a robust feature of successful endurance training.
